svg.title: Rotated array binary search illustration
svg.desc: An eight-step vertical walkthrough for searching key 3 in the rotated sorted array [5,6,7,8,9,10,1,2,3] using modified binary search.
svg.text: Binary Search in a Sorted and Rotated array
Single binary search on [5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 1, 2, 3] with key = 3
Each step keeps the exact reasoning from the original 8-frame walkthrough.
At every iteration, compute mid, detect which half is sorted, then decide whether the key can lie inside that half. Discard the other half.
Initialize low at the beginning and high at the end of the array.
Calculate mid = (low + high) / 2. Since arr[mid] >= arr[low],
the left half of the array is sorted.
As arr[low] > key, the key does not lie in the left half,
So move to the right half by updating low = mid + 1.
the right half of the array is sorted.
As key >= arr[mid] and key <= arr[high], the key lies in the right half,
So move to the right half by updating low = mid + 1.
the right half of the array is sorted.
As key >= arr[mid] and key <= arr[high], the key lies in the right half,
So move to the right half by updating low = mid + 1.
Calculate mid, as arr[mid] is equal to the key,
the key is found, and return mid as its index.
The key is found at index 8
